note: Producer vocabulary, layered onto the vendored UCS v8.2.1 synonym table at build time.
UCS is a sound-effects standard. Its synonyms were written for a library of doors and
footsteps, so a music-production library falls straight through it: no UCS subcategory
anywhere contains 'hihat', 'cymbal', '808' or 'oneshot'; 'hat' belongs to OBJECTS/FASHION
(clothing) and 'clap' to CERAMICS/IMPACT. A 36,864-file drum library scored 0.35% MUSICAL.
These entries are ADDITIVE and live outside UCS/categories/ on purpose: the vendored spec
stays pristine and upgradeable, and build.rs merges this overlay into the match index. The
keys are UCS category_ids, so nothing here can invent a category that UCS does not define.
Scope discipline: only words a producer would actually put in a file or folder name, and
only where the UCS subcategory genuinely is the right home for the sound. Adding a word to
a subcategory it does not belong in is worse than leaving it out — text evidence outranks
acoustic signal for most tiers, so a wrong synonym is a confident wrong answer.
